page: Amazon Web Services c6id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 virtual CPUs (16 physical cores with 2 threads per core) running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the c6id.8xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard compute and memory operations. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1658.0 and a multi-core score of 13971.0. In data processing tests, the instance registers a static web serving throughput of 34.06 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 31.19 million operations per second. For language model tasks, it demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small 135M models at 308.67 tokens per second, but drops to a weak tier of 6.24 tokens per second for prompt processing on large 70B models.

The c6id.8xlarge offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local storage. The inclusion of 1900 GB of NVMe SSD storage eliminates the need for external block storage for temporary data, improving qualitative cost efficiency for IO-sensitive tasks. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases. Recommended workloads include software compilation, web serving, medium-scale database operations, and text generation using small to medium machine learning models.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark data shows average performance across standard compute metrics, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13971.0 and a static web serving throughput of 34.06 GB/sec. The instance exhibits strong performance in text generation for small 135M models (308.67 tokens/sec) but shows weak performance for large 70B models. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed local storage alongside compute resources, such as software compilation, web serving, and database operations, though it is constrained by a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated vCPUs (Intel Xeon 8375C) at 3.5 GHz.
Equipped with 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Demonstrates strong text generation performance for small 135M LLM models.
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
